notes: SHIP v1.14.348 - ASSIGN RACK DESTROYED OTHER DEPLOYMENTS' RACKS. Owner: 'Ship the fix as .348. Take the rename too.' Found by the persistence sweep John ordered after .347, asking whether the partial-object save pattern existed anywhere else. It did not - but a DIFFERENT bug of the same root cause did. BUG (live, pre-existing, record-loss not field-loss): deploy_assignRack (L23713) loaded racks with deploy_loadRacks(deployId), which returns ONLY that deployment's racks (L25851 filters on r.deploymentId), then passed that scoped subset to deploy_saveRacks, which writes its argument as the ENTIRE DEPLOY_RACKS_KEY (L25853). Result: assigning a rack to a tech DELETED every other deployment's rack records - slots, currentPhase, assignedTo, powerCircuits, all of it - leaving those deployments' phases and optics orphaned under their own keys, pointing at racks that no longer existed. Needs 2+ deployments on one device plus one tap of Assign; invisible with a single deployment, which is why it survived. Root cause is the same as .347: reaching for a convenience accessor whose SCOPE does not match the write's scope. FIX (1 line of behavior): load the raw unscoped array via safeParse(localStorage.getItem(DEPLOY_RACKS_KEY)) + Array.isArray guard, matching the idiom every OTHER rack/phase/optic mutation path in the file already uses for exactly this reason (deploy_seedRacksAndPhases L27514-27515, deploy_advancePhase L27578+L27609, the gate-override path, deploy_seedOptics, deploy_updateOpticCount, blocker_save). Rack ids are globally unique ('rack_<deployId>_<idx>', L27519) so findIndex still resolves the correct rack from the full array - no cross-deployment collision. RENAME (owner-directed, the real prevention): deploy_saveRacks -> deploy_saveAllRacks at all 5 sites (1 definition + 4 call sites), so the scope asymmetry against deploy_loadRacks(deployId) is visible AT THE CALL SITE. Zero residue of the old name. ALSO FIXED, in the blast radius: the bare 'if (idx === -1) return;' immediately below was a No-Silent-Failures violation on a user-facing tap, and the unscoped load changes which array is searched - now console.warn + phantomToast('Rack record not found'). NOT DONE: deploy_savePhases / deploy_saveOptics / deploy_saveAudit carry the identical scope asymmetry against their own scoped loaders and were NOT renamed - owner asked for the one rename, and all their current callers are correct. Flagged for a follow-up ruling. No data recovery is possible for racks already destroyed by this bug. Gates: inline-JS vm.Script x3, CSS brace net-0 (no CSS touched), three-stamp .347->.348, surgical diff, rename residue check. DEVICE-VERIFY (clear SW first): with TWO deployments that both have racks, open deployment A, assign a rack to a tech, then open deployment B - B's racks are ALL STILL THERE (pre-.348 they vanished). Assign on a single-deployment device still works normally. ROLLBACK: revert this commit.
